    • Primeiros passos com o heatindexbr

Getting started with heatindexbr

Source: vignettes/getting-started.Rmd
getting-started.Rmd

heatindexbr provides spatially interpolated Heat Index (Rothfusz 1990) rasters at ~1 km resolution for the 1,477 municipalities of Brazil’s official Semiarid region (CONDEL/SUDENE Resolution 176/2024). The main product is a climatology for 2000-2025 covering all 12 months and 24 local hours. This vignette walks through the core workflow: search, download, extract, and plot.

Installation 

remotes::install_github("antcneto/heatindexbr")
library(heatindexbr)

What data is available?

Use hi_available() to see the products, temporal coverage, and available months and hours:

hi_available()
#>         product    period months  hours_local resolution n_files         format
#> 1   climatology 2000-2025   1-12         0-23      ~1 km     288 raster / table
#> 2 synoptic_2025      2025   <NA> 0, 9, 15, 21      ~1 km       4         raster
#>      status
#> 1 available
#> 2 available

Searching for municipalities

Municipality names follow the IBGE convention: uppercase without accents. Use hi_search() to find the correct name before calling other functions:

hi_search("Mossoro", state = "RN")
#>     code_muni name_muni abbrev_state
#> 500   2408003   MOSSORO           RN
hi_search("Campina Grande", state = "PB")
#>     code_muni      name_muni abbrev_state
#> 904   2504009 CAMPINA GRANDE           PB

Note: always use the name_muni value returned by hi_search() in subsequent calls. For example, use "MOSSORO", not "Mossoró". This limitation follows the IBGE naming standard.

Extracting Heat Index for a municipality

hi_municipality() downloads the municipal table on first use (~29 MB, cached locally) and returns summary statistics with NOAA classification. No raster download is required for this operation.

result <- hi_municipality("MOSSORO", state = "RN",
                          month = 1, hour_local = 15)
result
#>        code_muni name_muni abbrev_state month hour_local hour_utc  ic_mean
#> 139411   2408003   Mossoró           RN     1         15       18 35.20274
#>            ic_sd      noaa_class
#> 139411 0.3763849 Extreme caution

To retrieve all months and hours for a municipality:

hi_municipality("MOSSORO", state = "RN")

The noaa_class column uses the standard NOAA Heat Index thresholds:

Class IC range Risk
No caution < 27°C Low
Caution 27-32°C Fatigue with prolonged exposure
Extreme caution 32-41°C Heat cramps or exhaustion possible
Danger 41-54°C Heat exhaustion likely
Extreme danger > 54°C Heat stroke imminent

Downloading and plotting the raster

hi_download() downloads the full 288-band climatology stack (~1 GB) on first use and caches it locally. Subsequent calls read from the cache and extract only the requested bands in memory.

# Single band: January at 15h local time
r <- hi_download(month = 1, hour_local = 15)

# All hours for July (24-band raster)
r_july <- hi_download(month = 7)

# Full 288-band stack
r_stack <- hi_download()
hi_plot(r, title = "Heat Index — January, 15h local")

Note on hours: all hours are specified in local time (UTC-3). The conversion to UTC is handled internally. For example, hour_local = 15 retrieves the band stored at 18h UTC.

Extracting for a custom polygon

Use hi_shape() with any sf polygon object:

library(sf)
my_area <- st_read("my_area.gpkg")
result  <- hi_shape(my_area, month = 1, hour_local = 15)
result$stats
result$raster

Working with the 2025 synoptic product

The original 2025 product (four synoptic hours, annual means) remains available via the product argument:

r_2025 <- hi_download(product = "synoptic_2025", hour_local = 15)
hi_municipality("MOSSORO", state = "RN",
                product = "synoptic_2025", hour_local = 15)

Citation 

citation("heatindexbr")

Climatology dataset (2000-2025):

Campos Neto, A. (2026). Heat Index climatology for the Brazilian Semiarid (2000-2025): 1 km rasters, municipal table, and population exposure data. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21049066

Synoptic 2025 dataset:

Campos Neto, A. (2025). heatindexbr: Heat Index rasters for Brazil’s Semiarid region (v0.1.0). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20942619
